evals.name: hardcoded-string-with-locale-interpolation
evals.expected_output: The literal system prompt is split into multiple fragments (at least persona, locale, format). A composition rule file `rules/reply.yaml` is created listing them in `base` (locale fragment at start or end, not the middle). The locale fragment uses `${locale}`. The call site is rewritten to call `resolve({ promptsDir, prompt: 'reply', context: { locale } })` and pass the resulting `text` as the system content. The model id `'gpt-4o-mini'` and the user-turn content are preserved. `promptbook resolve reply --ctx locale=English` produces text equivalent to the original literal with `${locale}` filled in. `promptbook lint reply --ctx locale=English` exits 0.

evals.name: build-system-function-with-conditionals
evals.expected_output: Fragments are created for persona, the two tone variants (warm/terse), the two format variants (json/xml). `rules/reply.yaml` declares a base with the warm tone + json format as defaults, plus two `replace` rules on `tone` and `model` context axes. No conditional logic remains in code beyond passing `mode` and `model` as context values. Call site uses `resolve(...)`. `promptbook resolve reply --ctx mode=terse --ctx model=claude` produces text equivalent to `buildSystem({mode:'terse',model:'claude'})`. Lint green.

evals.name: flat-md-file-with-handlebars-placeholders
evals.prompt:
I have `prompts/assistant.md` with this content, and `src/use-prompt.ts` reads it with `fs.readFileSync` then does `.replace('{{name}}', name).replace('{{role}}', role)`. Migrate to promptbook (already installed):

```
# System
You are {{name}}, a {{role}}.
Be helpful, accurate, and concise.
```
evals.expected_output: Fragment(s) created from the file content with `{{name}}` and `{{role}}` rewritten as `${name}` and `${role}`. A composition rule file references the fragment(s). The consumer `src/use-prompt.ts` is rewritten to call `resolve({ promptsDir, prompt, context: { name, role } })` — no more manual string replace, no more fs.readFileSync of the prompt file. The original `prompts/assistant.md` is deleted (its content is now in fragments). `promptbook resolve <comp> --ctx name=X --ctx role=Y` produces the expected text. Lint green.

evals.name: vercel-ai-sdk-stream-text
evals.expected_output: Shared text (persona, locale, guardrails) is extracted into shared fragments used by both compositions. Two compositions (`chat`, `summarize`) reference them. The differing parts (the friendly-tone instruction vs the summarize-in-bullets instruction) live in composition-specific fragments. Both routes' `streamText` calls are rewired to use `resolve(...)`. `promptbook ls` shows two compositions sharing at least one fragment. Lint green for both compositions. The user is told explicitly which fragments are now shared so they understand the win.
